The court stated that it will issue an order to appear to the attorney. Subsequent to the hearing it was brought to our attention that a continuance had been requested and the accounting status report was continued to April 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-04-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for and with Monique Morrell. Dwight Morrell (Benificiary) was also present.

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that she filed an amended petition, a corrected notice and provided an endorsed filed copy of the supplemental waiver of bond signed by Ms. Williams (maiden name of Voncelle McClendon). In addition, she stated that the three Children were advised of their priorty to be appointed as the Administrators and their entitlement to statutory commissions. Mr. Morrell waived priority in open court. When directed by the Court to provide something in writing from the two remaining Beneficiaries with regard to waiving priority, Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that Ms. Williams was in a program, that she would not be able to have contact with her, that Ms. Williams stated that she did not wish to participate in the Court proceedings and assured the Court that Ms. Williams would not come back to object and cause additional litigation to the estate. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio also stated that there was urgency as the house was subject to foreclosure. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: R. Noguera/J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Garrido.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
